disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rough verrucous papules with disrupted skin lines and thrombosed capillaries (black dots), multiple/autoinoculated from picking — common warts (not molluscum's smooth umbilicated papules)
trigger_question_examples: Are they rough, dome-shaped lumps with tiny black dots and interrupted skin lines (rather than smooth waxy bumps)?
Have they spread since you started picking/biting them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, they're rough lumps with tiny black dots and the skin lines are interrupted, and they've spread since I picked at them.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No malignant features — no ulceration, irregular pigmentation, spontaneous bleeding, rapid growth or friability (screens SCC/melanoma), no secondary infection
trigger_question_examples: Is any lesion ulcerated, bleeding on its own, growing very fast, or darkly/irregularly coloured?
Is the surrounding skin hot, red, swollen or a red streak up the arm?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, none are ulcerated or bleeding on their own or dark/irregular, and the skin around them is normal.
